beats.text: This area in the monitored area was quiet through the previous day.
At 23:00 UTC the previous day, the system committed a public forecast: 75% jamming probability for 11:00 UTC — 12 hours ahead.
11:00 UTC arrived: GNSS integrity degraded exactly here. Verified hit — called 12 hours in advance, provably committed before the event.
This area in the monitored area was quiet through the previous day.
At 02:00 UTC the system committed a public forecast: 75% jamming probability for 14:00 UTC — 12 hours ahead.
14:00 UTC arrived: GNSS integrity degraded exactly here. Verified hit — called 12 hours in advance, provably committed before the event.
This area in eastern Europe was quiet through the previous day.
At 08:00 UTC the system committed a public forecast: 74% jamming probability for 20:00 UTC — 12 hours ahead.
20:00 UTC arrived: GNSS integrity degraded exactly here. Verified hit — called 12 hours in advance, provably committed before the event.
The record keeps its failures too. This point sits in the eastern Mediterranean.
At 00:00 UTC the interference channel lit up here. The 12-hours-ahead forecast had put the risk at just 1%.
Interference appeared here without a high-risk flag. It stays on the record — misses are published, never hidden.
